buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller cut Amazon 94%, rotating into semis, copper, and Argentine equities — AI mega-cap trade crowded.
Dalio: US equities approaching 1929/2000 bubble levels; national debt unsustainable; shift to boring fundamental trades.
Tudor Jones: S&P 500 buyers risk a decade of losses; long Bitcoin and AI infrastructure as selective hedges.
Gundlach: Warsh no dove — 1970s-style inflation means no cuts; rates stay higher, long gold and miners.
Burry shorting Nvidia, long undervalued healthcare; SpaceX at $3T is uninvestable.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): biggest bubble since railroads, only 4% of S&P 500 at new highs — March 2000 echo.
Rosenberg: stagflation incoming — inflation up, growth down, large parts of economy in deep pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — tech exuberance colliding with recession risk; crash parallels acute.
Yardeni: bull case intact, gains driven by FEMO not speculation; corrections are healthy.
Slok (Apollo): economy running hot, zero evidence AI is killing jobs — growth trajectory solid.
whats_new: Druckenmiller made a decisive 94% Amazon exit — sharpest mega-cap tech capitulation signal in the period.
Tepper rotated out of Microsoft (-82%) into high-growth AI memory — intra-tech rotation accelerating.
Ackman flipped: dumped Alphabet, went all-in Microsoft — quality-over-speculation thesis crystallising.
Gundlach flagging Warsh as hawkish surprise — market repricing of Fed pivot expectations now live.
vs_market_buy: Dalio/Burry see 1929-2000 bubble while market still prices ~21x forward S&P 500 PE — stark valuation gap.
Gundlach expects rates persistently higher; market still pricing meaningful cuts by end-2026.
Tudor Jones warns of decade-long S&P 500 loss risk; consensus earnings revisions remain upward.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees stagflation; credit spreads near 2-year tights imply benign macro — major disconnect.
Hartnett flags only 4% breadth at new highs while headline index near records — internal market stress unpriced.
Reid's crash-parallel warning sits against still-elevated equity risk appetite and low VIX regime.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Slok (sell): economy hot, no jobs lost to AI — bullish; Gundlach (buy): same hot economy means no cuts — bearish equities. Same data, opposite conclusions.
Visser (sell): forward multiples attractive on rising earnings — stay long; Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy): valuations are bubble-level — reduce equities. Sharpest directional split.
Yardeni (sell): FEMO drives gains, corrections healthy; Burry/Dalio (buy): growth priced for perfection, short high-valuation assets.
aligned: Dalio + Hartnett + Tudor Jones + Reid all independently invoke dot-com 2000 bubble parallels — market still rallying regardless.
Druckenmiller, Tepper, Ackman, and Berezin all rotating within tech — away from mature mega-cap toward semis, AI infra, and memory.
Gundlach and Reid both flag Fed/rates as the dominant macro risk, overshadowing AI narrative near-term.
buy_side_trades: Short Nvidia, reduce mega-cap tech broadly. Source: Burry (short), Druckenmiller (exit Amazon), Dalio (reduce growth).
Long Bitcoin and gold as inflation/dollar hedges. Source: Tudor Jones, Gundlach (gold/miners).
Long semis, AI memory, and AI infrastructure over software. Source: Druckenmiller, Tepper, Tudor Jones (IREN).
Long Argentine equities and copper — EM reform play and commodity cycle. Source: Druckenmiller.
sell_side_trades: Underweight broad equities; favour dividend/quality names over speculative growth. Source: Hartnett (BofA), Sonders (Schwab).
Long US equities vs short European equities on divergent growth. Source: Hatzius (GS).
Stronger USD positioning as unexpected macro trade. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Long duration bonds given rate-hike probability near zero. Source: Hatzius (GS), Wilson (MS — post-rate clarity).
dominant_themes: AI bubble valuation vs earnings-driven bull case
Fed path uncertainty — Warsh hawkish surprise risk
Inflation regime shift and stagflation tail risk
Mega-cap tech rotation into semis, AI infra, and EM

buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller dumped 94% of Amazon, rotating into semis, copper, Argentina — AI mega-cap crowded.
Dalio: US equities approaching 1929/2000 bubble levels; national debt unsustainable, cutting risk.
Gundlach: Warsh not an easy-money Fed chair; 1970s-style inflation makes rate cuts unjustifiable.
Tudor Jones: S&P buyers risk decade of losses; long Bitcoin and AI infrastructure instead.
Burry shorting Nvidia, long undervalued healthcare — growth/tech fundamentally disconnected from value.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): AI rally is biggest bubble since railroads; only 4% of S&P at new highs.
Rosenberg: stagflation in play — inflation rising, growth decelerating, large economy segments in pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — tech exuberance colliding with recession risk; crash parallels acute.
Slok (Apollo): economy running hot, zero evidence AI kills jobs — growth trajectory intact.
Yardeni: bull case intact via FEMO (earnings momentum), not speculative bubble dynamics.
whats_new: Druckenmiller executed dramatic 94% Amazon cut — largest reported single-name rotation of the period.
Hatzius cut US recession probability to 15% — material dovish revision vs prior higher estimates.
Warsh Fed narrative crystallised: Gundlach and Druckenmiller both flagging hawkish policy surprise risk.
Ackman flipped from Alphabet to all-in Microsoft — intra-mega-cap quality rotation now explicit.
vs_market_buy: Dalio/Tudor Jones call 1929-2000 bubble while market trades near historically elevated forward multiples — pricing disagrees.
Gundlach says no cuts justified; market still pricing eventual easing — duration positioning sharply at odds.
Burry short Nvidia as market prices AI capex cycle upside at peak multiples — direct valuation conflict.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees stagflation; credit spreads at multi-year tights imply no stress — stark divergence.
Hartnett flags 4% breadth and bubble parallels while S&P holds near highs — market ignoring internals.
Reid warns crash-precursor technicals; options markets pricing low realised vol — complacency embedded.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Slok (sell) says economy hot and no cuts needed; Gundlach (buy) agrees no cuts but warns of 1970s inflation trap — same rate view, opposite implications for equities.
Visser (sell) overweights Nvidia/Oracle on AI capex cycle; Burry (buy) actively shorting Nvidia as overvalued — direct opposing trade.
Yardeni (sell) calls gains FEMO-driven, not bubble; Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy) call explicit 1929/2000 bubble — fundamental disagreement on market character.
aligned: Dalio, Tudor Jones, Hartnett, Reid and Ackman all independently invoke dot-com 2000 bubble parallels — rare cross-camp consensus.
Gundlach (buy) and Hartnett (sell) both position for dollar strength and higher-for-longer rates — policy agreement.
Druckenmiller, Tepper, Ackman and Berezin all rotating within AI — away from obvious mega-cap toward semis, infra, and indirect plays.
buy_side_trades: Long semis, copper, Argentina; short/reduce mega-cap tech (Amazon, Microsoft). Source: Druckenmiller.
Long Bitcoin and gold as inflation hedges; long AI infrastructure (IREN); reduce broad S&P. Source: Tudor Jones, Gundlach.
Short Nvidia; long undervalued healthcare equities. Source: Burry.
Long Microsoft over Alphabet; long overlooked indirect AI beneficiaries. Source: Ackman.
sell_side_trades: Stronger US dollar positioning; safe dividend stocks; long Iran peace-deal beneficiaries. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Long US equities vs European equities on divergent growth; long duration bonds. Source: Hatzius (GS).
Long US equities on earnings momentum; monitor gold technicals. Source: Yardeni.
Position for equity rally post rate-clarity; exit traditional 60/40. Source: Wilson (MS).
dominant_themes: AI bubble valuation vs earnings momentum debate
Fed path uncertainty under Warsh — hawkish surprise risk
Mega-cap tech rotation into semis, infra, and emerging markets
Stagflation risk — inflation rising as growth decelerates
Geopolitical tail risk (Iran) — transitory vs structural inflation impact

buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller cut Amazon 94%, rotating into semis, copper, Argentina — AI megacap era fading.
Dalio warns US equities near 1929/2000 bubble levels; debt unsustainable, shift to 'boring' trades.
Gundlach: Warsh is no dove, 1970s inflation redux, rates stay higher — Fed trumps AI as macro driver.
Tudor Jones: S&P 500 buyers risk decade of losses; long Bitcoin and AI infra as inflation hedges instead.
Burry shorting Nvidia, long undervalued healthcare — growth multiples disconnected from fundamentals.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): AI rally is biggest bubble since railroads, breadth only 4% of S&P at new highs, echoes March 2000.
Rosenberg: stagflation incoming — inflation rising, growth falling, large swaths of economy already in pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — tech exuberance colliding with recession risk; S&P rally mirrors pre-crash setups.
Slok (Apollo): economy running hot, zero evidence AI kills jobs — no basis for rate cuts.
Yardeni: bull intact, gains driven by FEMO not bubble; market swoons are healthy, long equities through 2028.
whats_new: Druckenmiller made a decisive 94% Amazon cut this week — largest disclosed megacap tech exit of the period.
Gundlach explicitly reframes Fed Chair Warsh as hawkish, removing a key dovish catalyst markets had priced.
Hartnett flagged an imminent June-specific threat to end the rally — sharpening prior bubble warnings to a near-term catalyst.
Ackman fully exited Alphabet and went all-in Microsoft — quality rotation trade crystallised from prior hedged stance.
vs_market_buy: Dalio and Tudor Jones call 1929/2000-style bubble while S&P trades near all-time highs at elevated forward multiples.
Gundlach prices in persistent high rates with no cuts; market still discounts Fed easing later in 2026.
Burry shorts Nvidia as market prices AI capex cycle sustaining elevated semis multiples well into 2027.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees stagflation; credit spreads remain near 2-year tights, implying benign growth and contained default risk.
Hartnett's 4% breadth warning and bubble call contradicts a market narrative still pricing broad earnings expansion.
Reid's crash-precedent framing conflicts with consensus soft-landing pricing and sub-20% recession probability in rates markets.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Tepper/Visser both overweight AI infra; Berezin (sell-side) fades hyperscalers on unrealistic FCF versus $1T capex — same theme, opposite trade.
Slok (sell) says economy hot with no cuts needed; Gundlach (buy) also sees no cuts — but Slok is benign, Gundlach is alarmed by 1970s inflation trap.
Yardeni (sell) calls gains earnings-driven and healthy; Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy) call same rally a generational bubble — polar opposite frameworks.
aligned: Dalio (buy) and Hartnett (sell) both invoke 1929/2000 bubble comparisons — market is still rallying, dismissing both.
Gundlach (buy) and Reid (sell) both warn Fed policy will disappoint dovish expectations and pressure equities.
Multiple camps — Ackman, Berezin, Burry — independently flag AI speculation as misallocated versus underlying fundamental value.
buy_side_trades: Long semiconductors, short/reduce megacap software (Amazon, Nvidia). Source: Druckenmiller, Burry.
Long Bitcoin, gold, copper as inflation and dollar-debasement hedges. Source: Tudor Jones, Druckenmiller.
Long Argentine equities on structural reform; long AI infrastructure (IREN) selectively. Source: Druckenmiller, Tudor Jones.
Long undervalued healthcare; short speculative growth at peak multiples. Source: Burry, Ackman.
Position for higher long-end yields; long gold miners as rates-hedge. Source: Gundlach, Dalio.
sell_side_trades: Reduce broad equity exposure; rotate into dividend/quality names away from speculative AI. Source: Hartnett (BofA), Sonders (Schwab).
Long US equities versus short European equities on divergent growth trajectories. Source: Hatzius (GS), Yardeni.
Long duration bonds given rate-hike probability near zero; monitor for bond stress on debt concerns. Source: Hatzius (GS), Wilson (MS).
Long USD as most unexpected macro trade of 2026. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Fade hyperscaler valuations; buy AI supply-chain shortage beneficiaries. Source: Berezin (BCA).
dominant_themes: AI bubble valuation versus earnings legitimacy
Megacap tech rotation into semis, infrastructure, and real assets
Stagflation risk beneath surface-level growth metrics
Geopolitical tail risk (Iran) as transient versus structural inflation driver
